headline: Transcript: Trump changes tack on the Epstein files
summary: Marc Filippino talks to Chris Cook and Lauren Fedor
body_text:
This is an audio transcript of the Swamp Notes podcast episode: ‘Trump changes tack on the Epstein files’

Marc FilippinoWho knew Congress could act so fast?

[CLIP PLAYING]On this vote, the yays are 427, the nays are one.

Marc FilippinoJust a few hours after the US House of Representatives voted to release documents related to the late convicted sex offender, Jeffrey Epstein.

Markwayne Mullin voice clipThe bill is considered read three times and passed, and the motion to reconsider is considered made and laid on the table.

Marc FilippinoThe Senate waved through the same bill.

Donald Trump voice clipI have nothing to do with Jeffrey Epstein. I threw him out of my club many years ago ’cause I thought he was a sick pervert.

Marc FilippinoAnd US President Donald Trump’s sign-off was a bit surprising after we flipped back and forth on whether he wanted the documents to come out at all.

[MUSIC PLAYING]

This is Swamp Notes, the weekly podcast from the Financial Times, where we talk ab
